title: Drosanthemum asperulum
taxonomy: Drosanthemum asperulum belongs to the family Aizoaceae, commonly known as the ice plant family. This classification is due to its characteristics and growth habits that are similar to other plants within the Aizoaceae family. The Aizoaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, including Ruschioideae, which Drosanthemum asperulum is a part of. The Ruschioideae subfamily is characterized by its succulent plants, which is also true for Drosanthemum asperulum. The specific classification of Drosanthemum asperulum is still subject to ongoing research and updates in botanical knowledge.

side_effects: Drosanthemum asperulum is a succulent plant that, although it may not have any known severe side effects, its sap can cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals. intro: Drosanthemum asperulum, also known as the rough drosanthemum, is a succulent plant species native to the Western Cape province of South Africa, belonging to the Aizoaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and fever, due to its reported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Drosanthemum asperulum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including gastrointestinal issues and skin problems.
